subject: test+chore: pure helper extractions + comment trims
body:
Concrete actions from the "overall happy?" self-review.

Testing (actionable gaps from wrap #2)
- Extracted formatStorageSize + reconcileRemoveRow to
  src/lib/reviewHelpers.ts. Both were inline in views with no tests.
- Added 10 tests in src/lib/reviewHelpers.test.ts: KB/MB/GB boundaries
  for size formatting; tagInputIndex shift/clear/stable cases;
  currentPage clamp-to-bounds for removeRow.
- WorkspaceListView and ImportStepReview now import the helpers;
  ImportStepReview's removeRow collapses from 10 lines to 3.
- Test count: 135 → 145 (+10).

Skipped the radiogroup arrow-key focus test in TODO.md — mounting
WorkspaceForm for one assertion pulls in DateInput, router, the
validation composable, and `nextTick` focus semantics. Return on
investment is low vs. writing the plain test. Same story for the
debounced runway watcher and loadStorageUsage's async branch —
queued in TODO rather than force-fit into happy-dom.

UI smoke (wrap #1 — partial)
- Ran `vite dev` against the branch; server booted in 1050 ms and
  served index.html (9 KB). Strictly weaker than opening a browser,
  but confirms the module graph compiles in dev mode (not just
  build) — the HMR guards, async components, and template updates
  at least parse.

Comment trims (wrap #6)
- CashflowGraph chartSummary: 10-line Requirement/Approach/Alts
  preamble → 3 lines. Value of the sentence-form-over-data-table
  argument was low.
- useDialogA11y top docblock: 19 lines → 7. The "per-instance
  listener was the old design" line is the one load-bearing detail;
  the rest was restating Vue lifecycle.
- WorkspaceDashboard chart-split preamble: 6 lines → 3.

Left untrimmed: HMR dispose blocks (usePWAInstall, usePWAUpdate,
debugLog, useDarkMode), the chartSummary *function body* comment
on locale formatting, and TransactionEditModal isDirty comment
referencing the pure helper. All explain non-obvious WHY.

Not fixed: scope-creep framing in earlier commit messages (wrap
#1 on bundle-impact wording), mid-branch regression (wrap #3 —
history stands), and the a11y audits' surface-sampling nature
(wrap #5). None retroactively fixable without history rewrites or
additional audits.

Type-check clean. 145/145 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

Tags: tests, refactor, docs, cleanup
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-polish
Semver: patch
title: test+chore: pure helper extractions + comment trims
